topic: AI-driven, multi-step, continuous, agentic novel writing (2025-2026)

sources.title: RecurrentGPT: Interactive Generation of (Arbitrarily) Long Text
sources.key_claim: Simulates LSTM-style recurrence in natural language: short-term memory (10-20 sentences) plus long-term memory (paragraph summaries on disk + semantic retrieval) drives paragraph-by-paragraph generation with explicit per-step plan; treats prose generation as a step function with persistent state.
sources.relevance: Foundational architecture for any 'continuously running creative agent' — confirms language-as-state pattern works for long-form fiction. Directly applicable to canon-state design.

sources.title: DOC: Improving Long Story Coherence With Detailed Outline Control
sources.key_claim: Two-stage system: a 'detailed outliner' produces a hierarchical, fine-grained plan; a 'detailed controller' constrains generation to follow that outline — yields +22.5% plot coherence, +28.2% outline relevance, +20.7% interestingness vs Re3 baseline; humans rated DOC much more controllable in interactive mode.
sources.relevance: Demonstrates that hierarchical, controllable outlines are the SOTA-correct alternative to direct generation. Strong precedent for 'anchors + lens + branch promotion' interaction.

sources.title: Re3: Generating Longer Stories With Recursive Reprompting and Revision
sources.key_claim: Plan-Draft-Rewrite-Edit loop using a base LM and rerankers; +14% coherence and +20% premise-relevance over direct generation for 2k-word stories. Establishes the four-phase pipeline still inherited by 2025 multi-agent systems.
sources.relevance: Direct ancestor of every 'multi-agent novel writer' on GitHub. Useful as a baseline architecture to beat.

sources.title: Dramatron / Co-Writing Screenplays and Theatre Scripts with Language Models
sources.key_claim: Hierarchical prompt-chaining (Chinchilla 70B): log line -> title/characters -> plot -> location descriptions -> dialogue. CHI'23 evaluation with 15 industry pros: 84% found it useful, 77% reported true collaboration. Open-sourced colab + repo.
sources.relevance: Earliest credible 'serious creative agent' from a major lab; the hierarchical decomposition is canonical. Limited to ~screenplay-length output.

sources.title: PlotMachines: Outline-Conditioned Generation with Dynamic Plot State Tracking
sources.key_claim: EMNLP'20: introduces dynamic plot-state tracking conditioned on outline phrases; pre-LLM era but established that explicit state is needed because GPT-2/Grover alone produce incoherent narratives.
sources.relevance: Historical baseline; informs why pure long-context generation is insufficient even today.

sources.title: STORIUM: Dataset and Evaluation Platform for Machine-in-the-Loop Story Generation
sources.key_claim: 6,000 collaborative stories (~125M tokens) with character goals/attributes annotated; live evaluation platform where authors query the model and edit suggestions, with edit-distance metrics correlating with quality.
sources.relevance: The only public dataset that captures multi-author, persistent-character collaboration semantics — the closest existing analogue to author + creative-agent collaboration.

sources.title: TaleBrush: Sketching Stories with Generative Pretrained Language Models
sources.key_claim: CHI'22: line-sketch UI (drag a curve = protagonist fortune over time) controls a GPT-based story generator. Users iteratively sketch and read; control vs novelty trade-off measured.
sources.relevance: Strongest existing prior art for 'author intervenes via lens, not prose' — non-typed control surface for narrative shape.

sources.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (Park et al, Stanford/Google 2023)
sources.key_claim: 25 LLM agents in Smallville with observation/planning/reflection memory loop produce believable autonomous behavior; one seed (Valentine's Day party) propagates through the social graph over two simulated days. Open-source 'reverie' framework.
sources.relevance: Definitive prior art for 'always-running world simulator around the protagonist's contact graph' — directly applicable. Memory architecture (raw stream + reflections + retrieval) is canonical.

sources.title: Project Sid: Many-Agent Simulations Toward AI Civilization (Altera, 2024)
sources.key_claim: 10-1000+ agents in Minecraft with PIANO architecture (parallel information aggregation, multiple concurrent stateless modules sharing an Agent State); emergent specialization, rule-making, religion (Pastafarianism), and cultural memes. $9M seed.
sources.relevance: Demonstrates scale: 1000+ persistent agents with concurrent cognition+action is feasible. Useful upper bound on what 'persistent contact graph around protagonist' could achieve.

sources.title: Agents' Room: Narrative Generation through Multi-step Collaboration (DeepMind, ICLR 2025)
sources.key_claim: Specialized planning agents + writing agents (one per narrative section: exposition/rising/climax/falling/resolution); produces stories ~2x longer than end-to-end baselines. Introduces 'Tell Me A Story' dataset.
sources.relevance: DeepMind's official endorsement of multi-agent specialization for fiction; closest peer-reviewed precedent for the proposed architecture.

sources.title: StoryWriter: A Multi-Agent Framework for Long Story Generation (CIKM 2025)
sources.key_claim: Three-agent pipeline: outline agent (event-graph with character/event-event relations), planning agent (chapter-level event scheduling), writing agent (dynamic history compression). Generated 6,000 long stories (avg 8k words) and trained StoryWriterLLAMA / StoryWriterGLM via SFT.
sources.relevance: Open-source, reproducible multi-agent baseline that matches several requirements (event graph, chapter planner). Strong starting point if rolling-your-own.

sources.title: StoryBox: Collaborative Multi-Agent Simulation for Hybrid Bottom-Up Long-Form Story Generation (AAAI 2026)
sources.key_claim: Combines top-down storyteller agent with bottom-up multi-agent sandbox (World/Region/Zone/Area/Object 5-level hierarchy); produces 12k-word stories that beat all baselines on coherence, creativity, character development. Reference site: storyboxproject.github.io.
sources.relevance: Closest published analogue to 'always-running world sim that drafts chapters as canon stages settle' — the hybrid simulation+narration approach is exactly what this project envisions.

sources.title: Long Story Generation via Knowledge Graph and Literary Theory (arXiv 2025)
sources.key_claim: Multi-agent generator augmented with: (a) memory storage to prevent theme drift, (b) literary-theory-derived 'story theme obstacle' framework, (c) incremental knowledge graph for similarity computation, (d) writer-reader dialogue loop for revision. Beats prior outline-based methods.
sources.relevance: Closest precedent for using a structured prior (here: narratology) on top of KG. The architectural slot for narratology generalizes cleanly to Bazi/Qimen as the prior.

sources.title: BaZi-Based Character Simulation Benchmark (arXiv 2510.23337, 2025)
sources.key_claim: First QA dataset for BaZi-based persona reasoning across wealth/health/kinship/career/relationships. Symbolic-LLM hybrid (BaZi rule analysis -> reasoning -> scenario interpretation) achieves 30.3-62.6% accuracy lift over DeepSeek-v3 / GPT-5-mini. Wrong BaZi data drops accuracy 20-45% — proving it's load-bearing, not flavor.
sources.relevance: Direct evidence that Bazi-as-prior measurably improves character simulation. Validates the 'first-class probabilistic prior, not flavor text' design choice.

sources.title: Bazi MCP Server for LLM agents
sources.key_claim: Deterministic Bazi calculator exposed via MCP, handling DST and True Solar Time; ensures the LLM never hallucinates the chart, only interprets it. Designed for lifestyle apps, cultural platforms, and games.
sources.relevance: Production-ready calculation layer; can be reused as the deterministic side of the BaZi/Qimen prior. Avoids reinventing the chart engine.

sources.title: WebNovelBench: Placing LLM Novelists on the Web Novel Distribution
sources.key_claim: 4,000+ Chinese web novels; eight narrative-quality dimensions; LLM-as-Judge plus PCA aggregation. Benchmarks 24 SOTA LLMs and ranks them against human-written masterpieces, popular web novels, and slop.
sources.relevance: Only Chinese-web-novel-aware benchmark. Critical for evaluating xianxia output objectively.

sources.title: Lost in Stories: Consistency Bugs in Long Story Generation by LLMs (2026)
sources.key_claim: ConStory-Bench: 2,000 prompts, 4 task types, 5 error categories with 19 subtypes. Findings: consistency errors are most common in factual/temporal dimensions, cluster around the middle of narratives, occur in high-entropy segments, and co-occur. Releases ConStory-Checker for automated detection.
sources.relevance: Empirical motivation for structured canon + verification gates. Tells us where errors cluster (mid-story, high-entropy) so the canon-gate UX can prioritize those zones.

sources.title: BooookScore: A Systematic Exploration of Book-Length Summarization (ICLR 2024)
sources.key_claim: Compares hierarchical merging vs incremental updating for >100k-token book summaries. Hierarchical merging gives more coherent summaries but loses detail; larger chunks improve incremental updates.
sources.relevance: Directly informs the design of the persistent canon/memory book layer for long-running creative agents.

sources.title: NexusSum: Hierarchical LLM Agents for Long-Form Narrative Summarization (ACL 2025)
sources.key_claim: Multi-agent hierarchical chunking pipeline; up to +30% BERTScore over prior SOTA on book-length narrative summarization with scalable length control.
sources.relevance: State-of-the-art recipe for the 'compress canon into agent-readable form' subroutine.

sources.title: Weaver: Foundation Models for Creative Writing (arXiv 2401.17268, 2024)
sources.key_claim: Mini/Base/Pro/Ultra (1.8B-34B) family pre-trained on a writing-focused corpus and aligned to professional writers; Chinese:English = 4:1 in training data; Weaver Ultra beats GPT-4 and GLM-4 on writing style/creativity by their internal benchmark.
sources.relevance: Most credible Chinese-aware creative-writing foundation model; serious option for the prose-generation backbone.

sources.title: Sudowrite Story Engine 3.0 (commercial)
sources.key_claim: Braindump -> Genre/Style/Characters -> Outline -> Beats -> Draft. Generates 3,000-5,000+ word chapters from beats. Per current 2025-2026 reviews: 'fundamentally a scene-level tool you stitch into a book'; not a continuous pipeline.
sources.relevance: Most mature commercial fiction tool. Confirms the gap: even SOTA commercial product is on-demand scene-by-scene, not continuous.

sources.title: Novelcrafter (commercial; Codex + Plan + Matrix)
sources.key_claim: Codex (typed entries with aliases) attaches to scene-beat completions; matrix view sorts scenes by codex entry; Extract tool turns chat outputs into Codex/scene-summary entries. Drag-drop acts/chapters/scenes. Bring-your-own-LLM.
sources.relevance: Best 'world-bible aware' commercial workflow. The Codex pattern is reusable, but it's still author-typed and on-demand.

sources.title: NovelAI (commercial)
sources.key_claim: Llama 3 Erato 70B + Lorebook (typed memory); subscription $10-25/mo; positioned for free-form prose, anime imagery, and unfiltered content. Still turn-by-turn assistant, no autonomous loop.
sources.relevance: Strongest 'unfiltered' option for cultivation/xianxia content; weakest on narrative structure.

sources.title: Wavemaker (open source, free)
sources.key_claim: Free, cross-platform PWA for novel planning (snowflake method, storyboard cards, etc). Open-source v2-v4. Not AI-first.
sources.relevance: Reference for high-quality non-AI planning UX; useful for the planning/visualization side of the stack.

sources.title: Plot Bunni (open source)
sources.key_claim: Open-source novel-writing tool with AI brainstorming + drafting; works in browser on desktop/mobile.
sources.relevance: Lower-friction OSS alternative; not multi-agent.

sources.title: Squibler (commercial)
sources.key_claim: Templates + Smart Writer + 2025 'Book Proposal' phase (genre/tone/POV/key points before drafting). Generally noted as repetitive vs Sudowrite for fiction.
sources.relevance: Comparison point; not state-of-the-art for fiction.

sources.title: SillyTavern (open source roleplay frontend)
sources.key_claim: Character cards (.png+JSON), Lorebook, Auto-Continue (empty user message forces continuation), bring-your-own-backend. Best-in-class character-card ecosystem.
sources.relevance: Closest community-tested precedent for 'persistent character + lore' chat; limited as novel writer because output is dialog-flavored.

sources.title: AutoNovel by Nous Research (open source)
sources.key_claim: Inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch loop. Modify-evaluate-keep/discard with explicit propagation debts in state.json (lore<->outline<->chapter); LLM Judge + mechanical regex anti-slop checks. First novel produced: 'The Second Son of the House of Bells' (19 chapters, 79,456 words).
sources.relevance: The single most architecturally relevant open-source autonomous novel pipeline available in 2026. Anti-slop checks + propagation-debt model are reusable. English-centric.

sources.title: AI_NovelGenerator (YILING0013, GitHub, Chinese)
sources.key_claim: Chinese-first multi-chapter generator with explicit foreshadowing tracking (伏笔), character-arc state, semantic retrieval for long-range consistency, and contradiction-detection proofreading. Configurable LLM backend.
sources.relevance: Best-in-class Chinese-aware open-source novel pipeline. Treats 伏笔 as a first-class entity — directly relevant to xianxia where seeded foreshadowing is genre-critical.

sources.title: InkOS (open source) and AuthorClaw (open source)
sources.key_claim: InkOS: local web workbench, AI-detection, style analysis, human review gates. AuthorClaw: 'security-hardened' end-to-end fiction pipeline targeting KDP-ready manuscripts.
sources.relevance: Both inherit from AutoNovel-style pipelines. InkOS's 'review gate' aligns with the canon-gate UX; AuthorClaw's commercial framing is less relevant.

sources.title: AI Cultivation World Simulator (Steam + GitHub)
sources.key_claim: Open-world LLM-driven xianxia sim. Each NPC has its own LLM-backed personality, memory, goals; cultivation rules (spiritual roots, realms, techniques, lifespan, elixirs, spirit stones) are codified as constraints. BYOK (DeepSeek/Ollama/OpenAI compat).
sources.relevance: Closest existing implementation of 'LLM agents + cultivation rules as a coherent world model' — useful reference even though it's a game, not a writing tool.

sources.title: Cultivation World Simulator (4thfever / AI-Cultivation, GitHub)
sources.key_claim: Open-source agentic-workflow xianxia world simulator, dynamic emergent world, configurable.
sources.relevance: Codebase that already encodes xianxia-specific entities (sect, lifespan, techniques) — reusable as a starter for the world-state side.

sources.title: AutoGPT / BabyAGI / Auto-GPT critiques
sources.key_claim: Recursive task loops 'distract' on tangents (the 'shoelace' anecdote); confused by their own loops; 'autonomy' is shallow next-token prediction in a wrapper. Fiction-specific creative writing experiments existed but produced incoherent novel-length output.
sources.relevance: Cautionary baseline; argues why naive AutoGPT-style loops fail for fiction without strong canon/state and review gates.

sources.title: Character.AI: PipSqueak 2 (PSQ2) + Lorebook (April 2026)
sources.key_claim: Two-layer memory: short-term context + RAG/embedding long-term store; Pinned Memories + Memory Box; PSQ2 rolled out April 2026 to c.ai+, broader May 2026. Still session-biased, prone to drift.
sources.relevance: Confirms 'two-layer memory' is now industry-standard, but mainstream consumer apps still under-invest in true canon. We need to do better than this for novel-grade output.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Ultra (2M context) and Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M beta)
sources.key_claim: Gemini 3.1 Ultra ships 2M-token context (April 2026); Claude Sonnet 4.5 has a 1M beta via context-1m-2025-08-07 header. Both rated as best-in-class for coherent recall at 1M tokens, though structured retrieval still beats pure long-context for novel-length.
sources.relevance: Foundation: enough context now exists to keep an entire novel + extensive canon in-frame. This makes the proposed architecture practically feasible.

sources.title: Learning to Reason for Long-Form Story Generation (arXiv 2503.22828, 2025)
sources.key_claim: Adapts RL-with-reasoning to long-form story generation despite the absence of verifiable rewards / labeled completions; first serious attempt to bring R1-style reasoning to fiction.
sources.relevance: Relevant to 'agent that pauses for high-risk branches' — gives a recipe for an LLM that reasons explicitly about choices.

sources.title: Can LLMs Generate Good Stories? Insights and Challenges from a Narrative Planning Perspective (arXiv 2506.10161)
sources.key_claim: Benchmark using Answer-Set Programming for causal-soundness/character-intentionality/dramatic-conflict. Finding: GPT-4-tier models are causally sound at small scales but fail at planning with intentional characters and dramatic conflict; argues symbolic narrative planning still has a role.
sources.relevance: Empirical justification for keeping symbolic / structured planning (incl. Bazi/Qimen) alongside LLM generation. A pure-LLM pipeline cannot deliver the cultivation-novel beat structure.

sources.title: SCORE: Story Coherence and Retrieval Enhancement for AI Narratives (arXiv 2503.23512)
sources.key_claim: Incremental knowledge-graph construction for persistent story memory; multi-LLM-backend compatible; explainable industrial-scale narrative pipeline.
sources.relevance: KG-as-canon design. Strong fit for xianxia where lineage/sect/realm relations are graph-structured.

sources.title: Guiding Generative Storytelling with Knowledge Graphs (arXiv 2505.24803)
sources.key_claim: Editable KGs coupled with LLM generation; user-driven updates; Graph-RAG argued superior to vector RAG for long fiction continuity.
sources.relevance: Justification for KG backbone over pure vector RAG — directly applicable to canon design.

sources.title: Yuewen Group / WriteWise / DeepSeek for Chinese web novels (industry)
sources.key_claim: Yuewen has its own web-fiction-tuned LLM. WriteWise (Himalaya) for full-process authoring. DeepSeek widely used to draft 10,000-character chapters in ~1 hour for 起点 short-form satisfying novels (爽文). 38.7% of contracted Qidian works in 2025 are fantasy/xianxia.
sources.relevance: The Chinese-market reality: AI-drafted xianxia is already a commercial activity. Most authors use it for 'first draft fast', not for continuous autonomy.

sources.title: Building a 'Human-in-the-Loop' Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
sources.key_claim: Approval gates as 'predefined moments' where a human validates before continuing; non-blocking parallel-feedback patterns; UX rule: don't dump raw JSON, summarize.
sources.relevance: Direct UX guidance for the 'pauses for author decisions on high-risk branches' design.

sources.title: From Pen to Prompt: How Creative Writers Integrate AI (CHI Creativity 2025)
sources.key_claim: Interviewed 18 creative writers; they make deliberate decisions about *when* to invoke AI based on craft values (authenticity, voice). They reject 'AI does it all' workflows.
sources.relevance: Author psychology: even for power users, 'always-running creative agent' must respect intentional intervention. Validates the 'anchors/lens, not prose' design.

sources.title: PersonaGym: Evaluating Persona Agents and LLMs (Findings of EMNLP 2025)
sources.key_claim: Benchmark for persistent persona consistency in LLMs across multi-turn settings; LLMs exhibit measurable persona drift over long interactions.
sources.relevance: Quantifies the 'persona drift' problem that any always-running NPC/agent will hit. Argues for explicit persona/Bazi anchoring.

sources.title: CollabStory: Multi-LLM Collaborative Story Generation and Authorship Analysis (NAACL 2025)
sources.key_claim: Up to 5 LLM agents pass a story segment-by-segment; analyses authorship attribution across segments; finds strict turn-passing hurts coherence vs shared-canon designs.
sources.relevance: Confirms that naive 'crowd-of-authors' degrades coherence; you need a shared canon, not just relay.

sources.title: Multi-Agent Based Character Simulation for Story Writing (In2Writing 2025)
sources.key_claim: Director Agent + Character Agents respond chronologically along a scene outline. Targets 1k-2k token scene generation. Models a 'writers' room'.
sources.relevance: Lightweight, modular precedent for scene-level role-play that could plug into a continuous pipeline.

sources.title: LongStory: Coherent, Complete and Length Controlled Long Story Generation (PAKDD 2024)
sources.key_claim: Adds long/short-term context weight calibrator + structural-position discourse tokens; outperforms PlotMachines on coherence/completeness/relevance up to 10k tokens.
sources.relevance: Plug-in idea: explicit position tokens for structural state could be reused in a xianxia 'realm-stage' position embedding.

sources.title: StoryDiffusion (NeurIPS 2024) and StoryWeaver (AAAI 2025)
sources.key_claim: Consistent self-attention across long image sequences for character-consistent multi-panel/video storytelling.
sources.relevance: Adjacent — for cover art / illustration consistency. Not directly relevant to text canon but useful for ancillary product.

sources.title: Awesome-Story-Generation (curated GitHub list)
sources.key_claim: Most up-to-date public index of LLM-era story generation papers; useful as a watchlist.
sources.relevance: Single best entry point to track ongoing literature. Recommended for future review cycles.

sources.title: A Survey on LLMs for Story Generation (Findings of EMNLP 2025)
sources.key_claim: Splits the field into (i) independent LLM generation and (ii) author-assistance / collaboration. The 'always-running creative agent' sits *between* the two and is under-explored.
sources.relevance: Definitive 2025 academic taxonomy. Lets us position the proposed system precisely as a hybrid that the survey identifies as a gap.

sources.title: Narrative Theory-Driven LLM Methods for Automatic Story Generation and Understanding: A Survey (arXiv 2602.15851, 2026)
sources.key_claim: 2026 survey arguing narrative theory (Aristotelian, Freytag, Propp, etc.) is increasingly applicable to LLM extraction/classification/generation without large annotated datasets.
sources.relevance: Generalizes the 'theory-as-prior' design choice. Bazi/Qimen fits as a non-Western variant of narrative theory in the same architectural slot.
